title: Aureline onboarding task-success and first-useful-work measurement row
description: Boundary schema for the unattended onboarding measurement lane that audits whether protected dogfood onboarding paths (Open folder, Open workspace, Clone repository, Restore last session, Import from…) emit structured task-success and first-useful-work signals in a single shared vocabulary instead of a per-surface private notion of success. Every measurement row binds one onboarding path class to one entry-route id, one measurement surface, and one fuw_row corpus reference; declares the expected completion class, expected primary events, expected failure-category subset (from the closed surface vocab in docs/product/onboarding_measurement_plan.md §3), expected protected-metric refs, and a privacy-aware telemetry-default-posture; and pins a named failure drill that proves the lane fails loudly when first-useful-work is silently collapsed into mere app launch or when delayed success is classified as success. Adding a value to a closed enum is additive-minor and requires schema_version to advance; repurposing an existing value is breaking and opens a decision row in artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml.

measurement_row_id.description: Stable id of the form onboarding.measurement.<onboarding_path_class>.<slug>. No milestone slugs.

onboarding_path_class.description: The five protected first-useful-work onboarding paths the lane must measure distinctly. These do not collapse into a single 'first-run success' bucket.

completion_checkpoint_class.description: Which checkpoint the row's success criterion is qualified against. Lets the lane distinguish first-useful-work from mere app launch.

corpus_row_ref.description: The first-useful-work qualification corpus row this measurement projects against.

supporting_fixture_ref.description: Path to the worked fixture in the FUW case directory.

task_success_corpus_scenario_refs.description: References into the task-success corpus seed scenarios.

expected_completion_class.description: How the path resolves. Mere app launch does not satisfy any completed_* class; lanes that classify delayed_success as completed must surface check_id onboarding_task_success.completion_class.delayed_success_silently_classified_as_completed.

expected_failure_category_subset.description: Subset of the closed per-surface failure_categories from docs/product/onboarding_measurement_plan.md §3. Free-form categories are non-conforming; runner verifies the subset relation.

expected_primary_events.description: Subset of the closed per-surface primary_events from docs/product/onboarding_measurement_plan.md §3.

expected_protected_metric_refs.description: Protected metric refs the row qualifies against; runner checks they appear in the bound corpus row's protected_metric_refs.

telemetry_default_posture.description: Default telemetry posture the row asserts. opt_in_only is the canonical posture; emitters that ship default-on are non-conforming for this lane.

delayed_success_threshold_envelope.description: Optional descriptive envelope distinguishing on-time success from delayed success. The runner does not compute timings; thresholds are advisory placeholders until the benchmark council ratifies them.
